title: Kitty Endgame INIT-1 (clean retry v2): Builder closeout and operator authority
description: Clean retry of kitty-endgame-init-1-builder-closeout-v1 after B1-dogfood-preflight exhausted its 2-attempt budget on a repo:identity false-positive in ./kitty builder initiative doctor (worker worktrees were rejected by directory-name check). Root cause fixed in main at ebd1a93 (fix(builder): identify worker worktrees by origin), merged to main after v1 was applied, so v1's packets are permanently pinned to the pre-fix base_sha (manifests are immutable; base_sha is resolved once at apply time). v1 is paused, not deleted, as the audit trail. Packets below are otherwise identical to v1. Recommended worker tier for every packet: Luna medium reasoning. B2 is the authority gate: each concrete B3+ remediation must be added as its own immutable packet after the B2 audit names the gap, owner, and allowed paths. B7 and B8 are deliberately sequenced after the audit so they cannot grant execution authority outside verified Builder state.

packets.title: B1: Dogfood preflight and branch reconciliation
packets.objective: [Luna medium] Reconcile the live Builder queue, registered worktrees, current branch, and merged PR evidence before any Endgame dispatch. Write a concise evidence record under docs/initiatives/ that identifies the canonical next eligible packet, duplicates/superseded work, active leases, and any reason the closeout must stop. This packet is read-only with respect to Builder queue and Git state: it must not apply manifests, start attempts, mutate task state, create branches, or run packets.
acceptance_criteria: The evidence record names the source commands and their observed timestamp
The record classifies every currently queued or blocked packet as runnable, dependency-gated, superseded, or needs Jacob
The record names any duplicate initiative identity and recommends one canonical lane
No Builder queue, Git branch, worktree, or runtime file is changed

packets.title: B2: KB-S4 gap audit, docs only
packets.objective: [Luna medium] Audit the remaining KB-S4 / Builder closeout gaps using live code, tests, queue evidence, and the B1 reconciliation record. Write a docs-only gap register under docs/initiatives/. For each gap, state whether it is already landed, blocked on Jacob, unsafe to automate, or suitable for a new one-gap B3+ packet. Every proposed remediation must include exact allowed paths, dependencies, acceptance criteria, validation commands, and its execution authority boundary. Do not implement a gap in this packet.
acceptance_criteria: A gap register exists under docs/initiatives/ and is evidence-linked to B1
Each open gap is classified as landed, needs Jacob, unsafe to automate, or eligible for a one-gap follow-up packet
Every eligible follow-up has explicit allowed paths, acceptance criteria, validation commands, and dependency edges
No production, queue, or UI code is changed

packets.title: B3: Materialize one audit-approved gap packet
packets.objective: [Luna medium] Convert exactly one B2 gap marked eligible into a new, separately validated initiative manifest under docs/initiatives/. This is a manifest-authoring packet only: do not apply the new manifest, run its packet, or widen its allowed paths after validation. Additional B4..Bn remediations are intentionally not predeclared because their scope must come from the B2 evidence register, not a speculative umbrella packet.
acceptance_criteria: Exactly one B2-approved gap is represented by a new valid manifest under docs/initiatives/
The new manifest has a narrow objective, exact allowed paths, dependency edges, acceptance criteria, and validation commands
The new manifest validates with ./kitty builder initiative validate --json
The manifest is not applied and no queue state changes

packets.title: B7: Home Run next-packet POST and UI authority gate
packets.objective: [Luna medium] Add one explicit, audited Home Run action for the single next eligible Builder packet. The POST endpoint and UI button must be disabled unless the runtime manifest supplies an eligible packet and all authority checks pass. It must show the selected initiative/packet, require an explicit confirmation, record an auditable event, reject stale/paused/blocked/superseded targets with a specific error, and never choose a packet client-side. It must not bypass Builder's existing run-packet, worker, review, approval, publish, or queue protections. This packet must be coordinated with any active Builder UI work before execution.
acceptance_criteria: The server accepts only the current runtime-manifest next eligible packet and rejects client-selected stale, paused, blocked, or superseded targets with explicit errors
The UI names the selected packet and requires confirmation before POST
The action records an auditable Builder event without bypassing existing authorization or review gates
Route and UI tests cover success, no-eligible-packet, stale-target, paused-initiative, and authorization-rejection states
Relevant backend and frontend test slices and the production UI build pass

packets.title: B8: Initiative progress and next-action card
packets.objective: [Luna medium] Add a read-only Builder card that explains each initiative's state, progress, active/blocked packet, exact blocker, recommended next action, and who has authority to take it. The card must derive exclusively from the runtime Builder manifest; it must distinguish unavailable/stale/partial data from real queue state and must not imply authority that the backend does not expose. Any action affordance may only link to B7's audited flow when its server-side authority predicate is true. Coordinate with active Builder UI work before execution.
acceptance_criteria: The card renders active, completed, paused, blocked, failed, unavailable, stale, and partial-data states truthfully
Each non-complete initiative shows progress, the next or blocked packet, a plain-language reason, and an authority owner
No client-side inference upgrades a blocked or paused packet into a runnable action
The card has component tests for complete, active, blocked, paused, unavailable, and stale data
The production UI build passes
